purpose: Approved Agent Build Runner + Merge Gate policy for choosing exactly one approved agent-build backlog item, validating the required build/check/PR guard sequence, and refusing merge-ready status whenever checks are missing, failed, skipped, or unknown. This first implementation is a merge gate and readiness validator; it does not create agents automatically, run Codex, open PRs, approve, merge, deploy, or repair runtime files by itself.
selectionRules: Sort eligible entries by suggestedBuildOrder ascending.
Select exactly one entry per run.
Skip built, blocked, rejected, duplicate, superseded, and in_progress entries unless a later explicit resume task targets that exact entry.
Do not select high/critical protected-scope entries for runtime implementation; first versions remain docs/register/validator-only.
mergeGateRules: MERGE_READY must be false if any required check is missing, failed, skipped, unknown, stale, or not recorded.
MERGE_READY must be false if changed files match forbiddenFileScopes.
MERGE_READY must be false for protected runtime topics unless a later human-reviewed PR explicitly approves that exact protected scope.
MERGE_READY must be false when PR diff review, PR post-creation guard, auto-merge eligibility, or auto-repair decision evidence is absent.
A passing gate can only say the merge gate is configured; it does not merge by itself.
requiredOutputLines: Never merges PRs by itself: true

readinessSemantics.gateConfigurationReady: true means the report-only gate is configured, connected, and safe to run in quality-gate checks
readinessSemantics.mergeReady: always false in this report-only validator unless a future evidence recorder proves every required build and PR guard check passed
readinessSemantics.exitCodeRule: exit 0 when gate configuration is valid even though MERGE_READY=false; exit non-zero only when the gate policy, connections, hidden-character hygiene, or validator invariants fail
readinessSemantics.missingCheckRule: missing, failed, skipped, unknown, stale, or absent check evidence blocks merge-ready status but must not make the configuration validator fail by itself

hiddenUnicodePolicy.reason: GitHub flags files with hidden or bidirectional Unicode text; gate-owned files must stay free of format-control characters so reviewers can merge without hidden-character warnings.
